zh.lifeSummary: 當Constance Mowbray於1268年出生在英國越可爾哈茲列武德 煙雨 斯托利特斯時，她的父親Sir William MOWBRAY26歲，母親Mrs. Matilda De Mowbray of Axholme22歲。她和Sir Henry Vavasour I至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1345年在英國林可爾史爾盧斯享年77歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Constance Mowbray于1268年出生在英国越可爾哈茲列武德 煙雨 斯托利特斯时，她的父亲Sir William MOWBRAY26岁，母亲Mrs. Matilda De Mowbray of Axholme22岁。她和Sir Henry Vavasour I至少有2个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1345年在英国林可爾史爾盧斯享年77岁。

nameMeanings.name: Mowbray
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from Montbray (Manche). Robert de Mowbray was Earl of Northumbria 1086–95, but was later imprisoned and deposed for rebelling against William II.

variants.name: Mulberry
meanings: English (Middlesex and Cambridgeshire): variant of   Mowbray  .
Irish (Derry): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Maoilbhearaigh ‘descendant of the devotee of (Saint) Bearach’.

variants.name: Mayberry
meanings: English: most probably an altered form of   Mowbray  . It is also found as   Maybury  , which has the form of an English habitational name. There is a place near Woking in Surrey so called; however, this is not recorded until 1885 and is probably derived from the surname. In England this surname is found mainly in the West Midlands; it has also spread into Wales. In Ireland this form is common in Ulster; MacLysaght records that it was taken there from England in the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: Constance
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval form of the Late Latin name Constantia, which is either a feminine derivative of Constans ( see Constant ) or an abstract noun meaning ‘constancy’. This was a popular name among the Normans, and was borne by, amongst others, the formidable Constance of Sicily ( 1158–98 ), wife of the Emperor Henry VI .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Constance Mowbray was born in 1268, in Hazlewood with Storiths, Yorkshire, England, her father, Sir William MOWBRAY, was 26 and her mother, Mrs. Matilda De Mowbray of Axholme, was 22. She had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Sir Henry Vavasour I. She died in 1345, in Louth, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 77.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Constance Mowbray est née en 1268, à Hazlewood with Storiths, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir William MOWBRAY, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Matilda De Mowbray of Axholme, avait 22 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Sir Henry Vavasour I. Elle est décédée en 1345, à Louth, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 77 ans.

Sie hatte mit Sir Henry Vavasour I mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1345 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Louth, Lincolnshire, England.
